title: Effects of low-level prenatal lead exposure on child IQ at 4 and 8 years in a UK birth cohort study.
ehr_evidence: The study uses the ALSPAC birth cohort, which involves blood samples and cognitive testing, not electronic health records.
summary: Investigation of the association between prenatal lead exposure and child IQ at ages 4 and 8 in a UK birth cohort.
key_findings: No association of prenatal lead exposure with child IQ at 4 or 8 years in adjusted models.
Positive association for IQ at age 8 in girls.
Negative coefficients for IQ at age 8 in boys.
population: Pregnant women and offspring in the ALSPAC cohort (UK)
limitations: Small sample size of offspring at 30 months (n=235)
data_availability_statement: Data relating to prenatal lead exposure and child IQ at 4 and 8 years old in the Avon Longitudinal Study of Parents and Children doi:http://dx.doi.org/10.1016/j.neuro.2017.07.025 Data in Brief.
